perturbation.original_text: "Acceptance" and "Accept" have the meaning set forth in Article 3.4 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: "Acceptance" and "Accept" have the meaning set forth in Article 3.5 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction because the definition of 'Acceptance' is now pointing to Article 3.5, which discusses payment for milestones, instead of Article 3.4, which defines the process of acceptance and rejection. This creates confusion and makes it ambiguous how 'Acceptance' is determined, as other sections still refer to the original intent of Article 3.4 in relation to acceptance criteria.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the remedies set forth in Section 3.4, Conformis shall furnish Stryker with the Deliverables as defined in the R&D Work Plan, and Stryker shall have the opportunity to analyze and test each Deliverable for the purposes of determining Acceptance as set forth in the R&D Work Plan. Each Deliverable shall be deemed to be completed once such Deliverable has been Accepted (as defined below).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Acceptance' from Article 3.4 to Article 3.5. The contradicted text explicitly refers to Section 3.4 for determining 'Acceptance'. This creates a direct conflict because the procedure for acceptance is no longer defined in the location referenced by the contradicted section, rendering that section ambiguous and creating a contradiction regarding the method for determining Acceptance.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Improved Conformis Background IP and KIB Product IP ("Joint IP") shall be owned jointly by the Parties. Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Joint IP may be used freely by either Party or its Affiliates and licensed to Third Parties by Conformis and its Affiliates, on the one hand, outside of the Buyer Field or by Stryker and its Affiliates, on the other hand, within the Buyer Field, in each case, without the consent of, or duty to account to or notify, the other Party, but, except with respect to external licenses of the Improved Conformis Background IP by Conformis or its Affiliates to Third Parties, any external Third Party license shall be governed in accordance with the last sentence of Section 4.3(c) of the APA. Each Party to whom ownership is to vest in Joint IP by operation of law or by assignment by its employees or Agents agrees to assign and hereby assigns to the other Party an undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to all Joint IP; and to facilitate such assignment, the Party possessing such ownership agrees (i) to regularly ensure that its employees and consultants timely make any appropriate assignments to it; and (ii) at the other Party's reasonable request, to execute and have its employees and consultants execute, as necessary, all assignments and any other documentation to perfect the undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to the other Party of such Joint IP.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Improved Conformis Background IP and KIB Product IP ("Joint IP") shall be owned jointly by the Parties. Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Joint IP may be used freely by either Party or its Affiliates. Each Party to whom ownership is to vest in Joint IP by operation of law or by assignment by its employees or Agents agrees to assign and hereby assigns to the other Party an undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to all Joint IP; and to facilitate such assignment, the Party possessing such ownership agrees (i) to regularly ensure that its employees and consultants timely make any appropriate assignments to it; and (ii) at the other Party's reasonable request, to execute and have its employees and consultants execute, as necessary, all assignments and any other documentation to perfect the undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to the other Party of such Joint IP.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the licensing clause for Joint IP, specifically mentioning the Buyer Field division, from this section, it creates uncertainty about the rights of each party to license the Joint IP to third parties. This omission contradicts the implied understanding that both parties have specific rights to license based on the Buyer Field division. The phrase removal makes that specific section of the text, not have the proper legal standing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) All right, title and interest in and to the Improved Conformis Background IP and KIB Product IP ("Joint IP") shall be owned jointly by the Parties. Subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement, the Joint IP may be used freely by either Party or its Affiliates and licensed to Third Parties by Conformis and its Affiliates, on the one hand, outside of the Buyer Field or by Stryker and its Affiliates, on the other hand, within the Buyer Field, in each case, without the consent of, or duty to account to or notify, the other Party, but, except with respect to external licenses of the Improved Conformis Background IP by Conformis or its Affiliates to Third Parties, any external Third Party license shall be governed in accordance with the last sentence of Section 4.3(c) of the APA. Each Party to whom ownership is to vest in Joint IP by operation of law or by assignment by its employees or Agents agrees to assign and hereby assigns to the other Party an undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to all Joint IP; and to facilitate such assignment, the Party possessing such ownership agrees (i) to regularly ensure that its employees and consultants timely make any appropriate assignments to it; and (ii) at the other Party's reasonable request, to execute and have its employees and consultants execute, as necessary, all assignments and any other documentation to perfect the undivided one-half right, title and interest in and to the other Party of such Joint IP.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allows Conformis and its Affiliates to license Joint IP outside the Buyer Field and Stryker and its Affiliates to license Joint IP within the Buyer Field. The changed text removes this specific allowance for licensing based on the Buyer Field. The contradicted text is the same as the original text and thus contradicts the changed text by explicitly mentioning the Buyer Field division rights, which are no longer present in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Solely with respect to Confidential Information in which a Party has no ownership interest at all, owned or co-owned Confidential Information being exempt herefrom, upon termination of this Agreement, unless independently authorized to retain such of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information under an Other Agreement, each Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, cease all use and make no further use of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and shall, upon written request from the Disclosing Party, promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (including copies thereof) that is in tangible form (provided, however, that, with respect to electronic imaging of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, such materials shall be deleted and removed from access by an ordinary user from all computer hard drives, servers and similar media but shall not require any action to delete or erase such materials from any disaster recovery tapes or other back-up media or any record retention or computer storage system so long as the Receiving Party and its Representatives take such actions as are reasonably likely to prevent access to such materials by any person other than information technology and other administrative employees who are responsible for maintaining those disaster recovery tapes and other back-up media) and any documents created by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives containing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall provide to the Disclosing Party written certification of destroyed Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party promptly following the destruction thereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain one copy of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in a secure location in the Receiving Party's legal department for the purpose of establishing compliance with Applicable Laws (including professional standards) and for defending or maintaining any litigation (including any administrative proceeding) relating to this Agreement, the Other Agreement, the Prior CDA or the Confidential Information, provided that all such information shall continue to be kept confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Solely with respect to Confidential Information in which a Party has no ownership interest at all, owned or co-owned Confidential Information being exempt herefrom, upon termination of this Agreement, unless independently authorized to retain such of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information under an Other Agreement, each Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, cease all use and make no further use of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and shall, upon written request from the Disclosing Party, promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (including copies thereof) that is in tangible form and any documents created by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives containing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall provide to the Disclosing Party written certification of destroyed Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party promptly following the destruction thereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain one copy of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in a secure location in the Receiving Party's legal department for the purpose of establishing compliance with Applicable Laws (including professional standards) and for defending or maintaining any litigation (including any administrative proceeding) relating to this Agreement, the Other Agreement, the Prior CDA or the Confidential Information, provided that all such information shall continue to be kept confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific details of the handling of electronic imaging of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, specifically the part about disaster recovery tapes or other back-up media, there is now uncertainty about how the Receiving Party needs to handle that type of confidential data. This creates potential for misinterpretation and disputes regarding the Receiving Party's obligation to permanently delete or handle digital copies. By removing that part, it means there is no need for the recipient to follow the original digital policy/procedures, which creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Solely with respect to Confidential Information in which a Party has no ownership interest at all, owned or co-owned Confidential Information being exempt herefrom, upon termination of this Agreement, unless independently authorized to retain such of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information under an Other Agreement, each Receiving Party shall, and shall direct its Representatives to, cease all use and make no further use of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party and shall, upon written request from the Disclosing Party, promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (including copies thereof) that is in tangible form (provided, however, that, with respect to electronic imaging of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, such materials shall be deleted and removed from access by an ordinary user from all computer hard drives, servers and similar media but shall not require any action to delete or erase such materials from any disaster recovery tapes or other back-up media or any record retention or computer storage system so long as the Receiving Party and its Representatives take such actions as are reasonably likely to prevent access to such materials by any person other than information technology and other administrative employees who are responsible for maintaining those disaster recovery tapes and other back-up media) and any documents created by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives containing Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall provide to the Disclosing Party written certification of destroyed Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party promptly following the destruction thereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain one copy of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party in a secure location in the Receiving Party's legal department for the purpose of establishing compliance with Applicable Laws (including professional standards) and for defending or maintaining any litigation (including any administrative proceeding) relating to this Agreement, the Other Agreement, the Prior CDA or the Confidential Information, provided that all such information shall continue to be kept confidential pursuant to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies handling procedures for electronic imaging of confidential information, particularly disaster recovery tapes. By omitting this section in the changed text, there's now ambiguity on whether those procedures still apply. The contradicted text is the original version of the modified text, which provides specific instructions for electronic data, especially concerning disaster recovery and backup media. The omission of the specific guidance creates a direct contradiction, as the original intention and requirement for handling electronic data are now unclear, creating inconsistency, as described in the explanation and in line with the Omissions-In Text Contradiction definition.
